creator: trasz@FreeBSD.org
text: Hm, I've realized something else.  The board has two USB sockets, one fully functional, and one power only.  So, I basically did the same test as before, but with the other socket.  Electrically they are the same, it's just one of them doesn't have the data lines.  So, my theory went, if it was an electrical fault, then doing the plugging and unplugging dance with power-only socket would cause the same problems as before.  It didn't - everything is fine, the system works, not noticing any kind of activity on power-only USB socket.
